summary: Live npm publish token for @arbocollab scope shipped in tarball
details: The package tarball includes `npmjs.npmrc` containing a live npm auth token (`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`) mapped to the npm registry. `package.json` declares `"files": ["*"]` and `.npmignore` does not exclude `npmjs.npmrc`, so the file is published verbatim. Any party who installs or downloads this package obtains a working publish credential for the `@arbocollab` scope, allowing them to publish malicious versions of any package in that scope and attack downstream consumers. This is credential redistribution affecting a third-party system (the npm registry's @arbocollab namespace), not author self-harm in the narrow sense — installers gain attack capability against other installers via the scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
